{"key_event":"Post-Mao recentralization and reform opening","reasoning":"After Mao, the Party-state restored ministries, courts, schools, cadre systems, diplomatic reach, and centralized coercive control, raising state capacity despite lingering bureaucratic and military weaknesses. Civil society remained highly constrained after the suppression of mass factional politics and Democracy Wall, but early market reforms, revived education, foreign contact, and peasant household autonomy modestly widened social space.","society_change":0.7,"society_power":2.0,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":8.6}
